LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC, CO.'S DEMURRER IS: A) OVERRULED AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WRONGFUL TERMINATION IN VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY AND B) SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIFTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AND VIOLATION OF DUE PROCESS AS TO PG&E. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 =302/HK

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT KATHLEEN KAY'S DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO FIRST, SECOND, THIRD, FIFTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR DISCRIMINATION IN VIOLATION OF FEHA, WRONGFUL TERMINATION IN VIOLATION OF FEHA, WRONGFUL TERMINATION IN VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY, INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AND VIOLATION OF DUE PROCESS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-31-2018 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-15-2018 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jun-12-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-16-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert D. Eassa, Esq. of Duane Morris, T:415.957.3091, for defendant/moving party;
Richard L. Richardson, Esq. of Siegal & Richardson LLP, T:415.651.1949, for plaintiff/opposing party.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT KATHLEEN KAY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT as follows:

Defendant Kathleen Kay's motion for summary judgment is granted. Viewing all the evidence in a light most favorable to plaintiff Leticia Dixon, that evidence does not show either severe or pervasive harassing conduct. Most of the alleged harassment consists of personnel decisions which do not constitute actionable harassment. While Ms. Kay's statement that she felt unsafe and uncomfortable in Ms. Dixon's presence is evidence of racial animus, it is not "severe" to support a race harassment claim.

ORDER SIGNED after hearing. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-16-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert D. Eassa, Esq. of Duane Morris, T:415.957.3091, for defendant/moving party;
Richard L. Richardson, Esq. of Siegal & Richardson LLP, T:415.651.1949, for plaintiff/opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication, as follows:

Defendant Pacific Gas And Electric Company's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternative motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the fourth cause of action for race harassment and denied as to the first through third causes of action for race discrimination. There is a triable dispute whether the termination of plaintiff Leticia Dixon's employment was substantially motivated by discriminatory animus against Ms. Dixon. A reasonable juror could find in favor of Ms. Dixon on her discrimination claims based on: 1) Defendant Kathleen Kay's statement that she felt unsafe and uncomfortable in the presence of Ms. Dixon, 2) the evidence contradicting material statements made by Ms. Kay to the internal investigator, 3) the fact that Ms. Dixon was never shown the report of the external investigator, and 4) the notes made by Ms. Kay recording transgressions of Ms. Dixon. PG&E is entitled to a summary adjudication on Ms. Dixon's harassment claim because, even viewing all the evidence in a light most favorable to Ms. Dixon, that evidence does not show either severe or pervasive harassing conduct. Most of the alleged harassment consists of personnel decisions which do not constitute actionable harassment. While Ms. Kay's statement that she felt unsafe and uncomfortable in Ms. Dixon's presence is evidence of racial animus, it is not "severe" to support a race harassment claim.

Mr. Richardson to prepare and submit a proposed order which complies with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter CSR#6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-09-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Kathleen Kay's demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to first, second, third, fifth and sixth causes of action for discrimination in violation of FEHA, wrongful termination in violation of FEHA, wrongful termination in violation of public policy, intentional infliction of emotional distress and violation of due process. As to the first, second, and third causes of action, Ms. Kay cannot be held individually liable as a manager or supervisor under FEHA for alleged discriminatory acts or for wrongful discharge in violation of public policy. (Reno v. Baird (1998) 18 Cal.4th 640, 663.) As to the fifth cause of action, the complaint fails to state any facts giving rise to personal liability by Ms. Kay and it is barred by the workers' compensation exclusivity rule. By her failure to address the demurrer to her sixth cause of action, Ms. Dixon concedes that her sixth cause of action is deficient as a matter of law, and it is. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-09-2017 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - Defendant Pacific Gas and Electric, Co.'s demurrer is: a) overruled as to the third cause of action for wrongful termination in violation of public policy and b) sustained without leave to amend as to the fifth and sixth causes of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress and violation of due process as to PG&E. As to the third cause of action, plaintiff Leticia Dixon adequately alleges a claim for wrongful termination in violation of public policy. While the complaint should have expressly alleged that the public policies were founded on FEHA and the provision in the California Constitution prohibiting sex discrimination in employment, a fair reading of the complaint shows that is what is intended. No useful purpose would be served by requiring Ms. Dixon to amend her complaint to make these allegations. As to the fifth cause of action, while there is case law that a Tameny claim seeking emotional distress damages is not barred the workers' compensation exclusivity rule, there is no authority stating that an IIED claim is also outside that rule. In all events, to the extent that Ms. Dixon seeks to save her IIED claim from the bar of the exclusivity rule, that claim is substantively identical to her third casue of action and thus there is no reason she should proceed on a separate IIED claim. By her failure to address the demurrer to her sixth cause of action, Ms. Dixon concedes that her sixth cause of action is deficient as a matter of law, and it is. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =302/hk
